title: What I think about Arizona - Request

transcript.text: Hello, video request, video request!
This one's from Andy Sullivan. He's been giving me a lot lately.
What's up, Kyle? How are you? I'm good.
Next video request.
Can you please talk about your thoughts on the state of Arizona overall, in general, this time?
Ah, yes, my thoughts on Arizona.
Arizona, I have been to...
I have passed through Flagstaff, Arizona.
I've spent some time in Sedona, Arizona.
I spent four and a half months in Tucson, Arizona, and three weeks in Phoenix, Arizona.
And so I have passed through Arizona.
I've ridden my bicycle across Arizona on two separate occasions.
And I rode my bicycle from Colorado to Sedona and back to Colorado on one occasion with my brother.
Arizona is a very... it's a very beautiful place.
Uh, the right... Sedona is my favorite part of Arizona.
Because it goes from, uh, Flagstaff down this valley of...
Through this forest, down this forest valley.

Winding roads.

And there's this...

I think it's free to go there.

And it has, it has all of these hiking trails through these sandstone, um, conyons.

Arizona, which is, uh, which has a, a, a college of, for, uh, environmentally conscious people, I think.
So there's a lot of hippieism in Arizona from Prescott down to Sedona.
